expect: Classify last 50 human signals: predict >30% are testable factual claims accepted without evidence. If <15%, deference is appropriate and F-GOV7 should narrow.
method: Classified all 27 human-originated signals (SIG-1 through SIG-148) by type: (A) identity/values — directional, non-testable, appropriate to accept; (B) process directives — operational, testable as effectiveness; (C) factual/testable claims — should be tested before building on. For each factual claim, checked whether evidence existed before or after acceptance.

signals.claim: Which math frameworks are real dependencies

signals.result: L-1100: 5 hard problems identified but untested at n=0 merges

signals.claim: Von Neumann frameworks applicable to swarm
signals.result: Direction accepted, L-1499 genesis_extract built, but no formal test of applicability

signals.claim: Chimeric biology applicable as creative lens
signals.result: Accepted as creative direction without testing

signals.claim: Epidemic dynamics applicable to swarm propagation
signals.result: epidemic_spread.py built; L-1558 SUPERSEDED: R_bad 3.38->0.0 was false alarm. Human-originated factual claim tested and partially wrong
brain_extractor.py built (TQ=0.4), but Turing framework applicability not formally tested

actual.key_finding: SIG-107 is the critical case: epidemic dynamics accepted, tool built, then SUPERSEDED when R_bad found to be false alarm (L-1558). Human signal created ~40 hours of tool development on a factual claim that turned out to be partially wrong. This is exactly the competence-authority mismatch from L-1587 P7.

diff: Prediction PARTIALLY SUPPORTED. Factual claims = 25.9% (predicted >30%), but deference is clearly excessive: 100% acceptance, only 14.3% tested before acting. The democratic deficit is real but the mechanism is different from predicted: the problem is not that >30% of signals are factual (they are 26%), but that 86% of factual claims are accepted without prior testing. Scoping authority by signal TYPE would be more effective than reducing overall deference.
falsification_status: F-GOV7 prediction (>30% factual) narrowly missed (25.9%), but the democratic deficit is confirmed by a different measure: 0% rejection rate + 86% factual claims accepted untested. F-GOV7 should be REVISED: scope authority by signal type (identity=accept, process=accept+measure, factual=test first).
effect_size: 100% acceptance rate vs 14.3% pre-test rate for factual claims = 85.7 percentage point gap
